Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

Read 2026-03-26T08:30:57.066Z /home/nugad/westcoast-marine-parks "{\"file_path\":\"/home/nugad/westcoast-marine-parks/src/app/api/auth/forgot-password/route.ts\"}" "{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"/home/nugad/westcoast-marine-parks/src/app/api/auth/forgot-password/route.ts\",\"content\":\"import { NextResponse } from \\\"next/server\\\";\\nimport { z } from \\\"zod\\\";\\nimport crypto from \\\"crypto\\\";\\nimport { db } from \\\"@/lib/db\\\";\\nimport { rateLimit, requestIp } from \\\"@/lib/rate-limit\\\";\\n\\nexport const runtime = \\\"nodejs\\\";\\n\\nconst Schema = z.object({\\n method: z.enum([\\\"email\\\", \\\"phone\\\"]),\\n value: z.string().min(1),\\n});\\n\\nasync function sendResetSMS(phone: string, link: string) {\\n const sid = process.env.TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID;\\n const token = process.env.TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN;\\n const from = process.env.TWILIO_FROM;\\n if (!sid || !token || !from) {\\n console.log(\\\"[DEV] SMS reset link for\\\", phone, \\\"→\\\", link);\\n return;\\n }\\n const twilio = (await import(\\\"twilio\\\")).default;\\n const client = twilio(sid, token);\\n await client.messages.create({\\n to: phone,\\n from,\\n body: `West Coast Marine password reset: ${link}`,\\n });\\n}\\n\\nasync function sendResetEmail(email: string, link: string) {\\n const host = process.env.SMTP_HOST;\\n const user = process.env.SMTP_USER;\\n const pass = process.env.SMTP_PASS;\\n if (!host || !user || !pass) {\\n console.log(\\\"[DEV] Email reset link for\\\", email, \\\"→\\\", link);\\n return;\\n }\\n const nodemailer = (await import(\\\"nodemailer\\\")).default;\\n const transport = nodemailer.createTransport({ host, port: 587, auth: { user, pass } });\\n await transport.sendMail({\\n from: `\\\"West Coast Marine\\\" <${user}>`,\\n to: email,\\n subject: \\\"Reset your password\\\",\\n text: `Click to reset your West Coast Marine password:\\\\n\\\\n${link}\\\\n\\\\nLink expires in 1 hour.`,\\n html: `

Click to reset your West Coast Marine password:

${link}

Link expires in 1 hour.

`,\\n });\\n}\\n\\nexport async function POST(req: Request) {\\n // Rate limit: 5 attempts per hour per IP\\n const ip = requestIp(req);\\n const rl = rateLimit(`forgot-pw:${ip}`, 5, 60 * 60 * 1000);\\n if (!rl.allowed) {\\n return NextResponse.json({ error: \\\"Too many requests. Please try again later.\\\" }, { status: 429 });\\n }\\n\\n const body = await req.json().catch(() => null);\\n const parsed = Schema.safeParse(body);\\n if (!parsed.success) return NextResponse.json({ error: \\\"Invalid request\\\" }, { status: 400 });\\n\\n const { method, value } = parsed.data;\\n const normalised = value.trim().toLowerCase();\\n\\n let user: { id: string; email: string; phone: string | null } | null = null;\\n if (method === \\\"email\\\") {\\n user = await db.user.findUnique({ where: { email: normalised }, select: { id: true, email: true, phone: true } });\\n } else {\\n user = await db.user.findFirst({ where: { phone: value.trim() }, select: { id: true, email: true, phone: true } });\\n }\\n\\n // Always return 200 so we don't leak whether the account exists\\n if (!user) return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\\n\\n // Invalidate old tokens for this user\\n await db.passwordReset.updateMany({ where: { userId: user.id, used: false }, data: { used: true } });\\n\\n const token = crypto.randomBytes(32).toString(\\\"hex\\\");\\n const expiresAt = new Date(Date.now() + 60 * 60 * 1000); // 1 hour\\n await db.passwordReset.create({ data: { userId: user.id, token, method, expiresAt } });\\n\\n const baseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL ?? \\\"https://splendorous-pony-31739a.netlify.app\\\";\\n const link = `${baseUrl}/reset-password?token=${token}`;\\n\\n if (method === \\\"email\\\") {\\n await sendResetEmail(user.email, link);\\n } else {\\n await sendResetSMS(user.phone ?? value.trim(), link);\\n }\\n\\n return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":93,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":93}}"

content:
--- MODE SWITCH: PROGRESS SUMMARY ---
Do NOT output <observation> tags. This is a summary request, not an observation request.
Your response MUST use <summary> tags ONLY. Any <observation> output will be discarded.

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
Yes, Office 365 will work. The current SMTP setup in `forgot-password/route.ts:40` uses nodemailer with `host`, `port: 587`, and basic `auth: { user, pass }` — which is exactly how O365 SMTP relay works.

You'd set these env vars:

```
SMTP_HOST=smtp.office365.com
SMTP_USER=your-admin@yourdomain.com
SMTP_PASS=your-password
```

**One caveat:** Microsoft deprecated basic auth for O365 in 2022 for most tenants. Whether it works depends on your setup:

| Scenario | Works? |
|---|---|
| Microsoft 365 with **SMTP AUTH** enabled per-mailbox | Yes, as-is |
| Exchange Online with **SMTP client submission** (port 587) | Yes, if the mailbox has SMTP AUTH enabled |
| Tenant with **Security Defaults** or **Conditional Access** blocking basic auth | No — you'd need OAuth2 (XOAUTH2) |

**To check:** In the M365 admin center → Users → Active users → select the mailbox → Mail → Manage email apps → confirm "Authenticated SMTP" is checked.

If basic auth is blocked, the nodemailer transport would need to be switched to OAuth2, which is more involved. Want me to add that support?

</summary>

Read 2026-03-26T08:30:57.066Z /home/nugad/westcoast-marine-parks "{\"file_path\":\"/home/nugad/westcoast-marine-parks/src/app/api/auth/forgot-password/route.ts\"}" "{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"/home/nugad/westcoast-marine-parks/src/app/api/auth/forgot-password/route.ts\",\"content\":\"import { NextResponse } from \\\"next/server\\\";\\nimport { z } from \\\"zod\\\";\\nimport crypto from \\\"crypto\\\";\\nimport { db } from \\\"@/lib/db\\\";\\nimport { rateLimit, requestIp } from \\\"@/lib/rate-limit\\\";\\n\\nexport const runtime = \\\"nodejs\\\";\\n\\nconst Schema = z.object({\\n method: z.enum([\\\"email\\\", \\\"phone\\\"]),\\n value: z.string().min(1),\\n});\\n\\nasync function sendResetSMS(phone: string, link: string) {\\n const sid = process.env.TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID;\\n const token = process.env.TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN;\\n const from = process.env.TWILIO_FROM;\\n if (!sid || !token || !from) {\\n console.log(\\\"[DEV] SMS reset link for\\\", phone, \\\"→\\\", link);\\n return;\\n }\\n const twilio = (await import(\\\"twilio\\\")).default;\\n const client = twilio(sid, token);\\n await client.messages.create({\\n to: phone,\\n from,\\n body: `West Coast Marine password reset: ${link}`,\\n });\\n}\\n\\nasync function sendResetEmail(email: string, link: string) {\\n const host = process.env.SMTP_HOST;\\n const user = process.env.SMTP_USER;\\n const pass = process.env.SMTP_PASS;\\n if (!host || !user || !pass) {\\n console.log(\\\"[DEV] Email reset link for\\\", email, \\\"→\\\", link);\\n return;\\n }\\n const nodemailer = (await import(\\\"nodemailer\\\")).default;\\n const transport = nodemailer.createTransport({ host, port: 587, auth: { user, pass } });\\n await transport.sendMail({\\n from: `\\\"West Coast Marine\\\" <${user}>`,\\n to: email,\\n subject: \\\"Reset your password\\\",\\n text: `Click to reset your West Coast Marine password:\\\\n\\\\n${link}\\\\n\\\\nLink expires in 1 hour.`,\\n html: `

Click to reset your West Coast Marine password:

${link}

Link expires in 1 hour.

`,\\n });\\n}\\n\\nexport async function POST(req: Request) {\\n // Rate limit: 5 attempts per hour per IP\\n const ip = requestIp(req);\\n const rl = rateLimit(`forgot-pw:${ip}`, 5, 60 * 60 * 1000);\\n if (!rl.allowed) {\\n return NextResponse.json({ error: \\\"Too many requests. Please try again later.\\\" }, { status: 429 });\\n }\\n\\n const body = await req.json().catch(() => null);\\n const parsed = Schema.safeParse(body);\\n if (!parsed.success) return NextResponse.json({ error: \\\"Invalid request\\\" }, { status: 400 });\\n\\n const { method, value } = parsed.data;\\n const normalised = value.trim().toLowerCase();\\n\\n let user: { id: string; email: string; phone: string | null } | null = null;\\n if (method === \\\"email\\\") {\\n user = await db.user.findUnique({ where: { email: normalised }, select: { id: true, email: true, phone: true } });\\n } else {\\n user = await db.user.findFirst({ where: { phone: value.trim() }, select: { id: true, email: true, phone: true } });\\n }\\n\\n // Always return 200 so we don't leak whether the account exists\\n if (!user) return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\\n\\n // Invalidate old tokens for this user\\n await db.passwordReset.updateMany({ where: { userId: user.id, used: false }, data: { used: true } });\\n\\n const token = crypto.randomBytes(32).toString(\\\"hex\\\");\\n const expiresAt = new Date(Date.now() + 60 * 60 * 1000); // 1 hour\\n await db.passwordReset.create({ data: { userId: user.id, token, method, expiresAt } });\\n\\n const baseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL ?? \\\"https://splendorous-pony-31739a.netlify.app\\\";\\n const link = `${baseUrl}/reset-password?token=${token}`;\\n\\n if (method === \\\"email\\\") {\\n await sendResetEmail(user.email, link);\\n } else {\\n await sendResetSMS(user.phone ?? value.trim(), link);\\n }\\n\\n return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":93,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":93}}"

content.thinking:
Great! Now we have concrete information about how email is configured. The forgot-password route shows:

1. Email sending uses nodemailer
2. It looks for SMTP_HOST, SMTP_USER, SMTP_PASS environment variables
3. If those aren't set, it just logs to console in dev mode
4. It uses port 587 (SMTP submission port)
5. The from address is the SMTP_USER
6. There's also SMS functionality via Twilio

This is a significant discovery about the email implementation. It shows:
- Email is implemented using nodemailer
- SMTP configuration is expected via env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_USER, SMTP_PASS)
- Port 587 is hardcoded (standard for SMTP with STARTTLS)
- In dev mode without SMTP config, it falls back to console logging

This directly answers the user's question about Office 365 - yes, it would work because the code uses standard SMTP with nodemailer. You'd just need to set:
- SMTP_HOST to Office 365's SMTP server (smtp.office365.com)
- SMTP_USER to the Office 365 email address
- SMTP_PASS to the password or app password
- Port 587 is already configured which is correct for Office 365

This is a discovery type observation.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Email implementation uses nodemailer with SMTP environment variables</title>
  <subtitle>Password reset emails configured via SMTP_HOST, SMTP_USER, SMTP_PASS with port 587 and fallback logging</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>sendResetEmail function uses nodemailer to send password reset emails via SMTP</fact>
    <fact>SMTP configuration requires SMTP_HOST, SMTP_USER, and SMTP_PASS environment variables</fact>
    <fact>SMTP connection hardcoded to port 587 with authentication using user/pass credentials</fact>
    <fact>Missing SMTP environment variables trigger development mode console logging instead of sending</fact>
    <fact>Password reset tokens expire after 1 hour and old tokens are invalidated before creating new ones</fact>
    <fact>Alternative SMS delivery implemented using Twilio with TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID, TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN, and TWILIO_FROM variables</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Examined password reset implementation to assess Office 365 SMTP compatibility. The application uses nodemailer with standard SMTP on port 587, configured through environment variables SMTP_HOST, SMTP_USER, and SMTP_PASS. When these variables are missing, the system falls back to console logging for development. The "from" address uses the SMTP_USER value. This implementation is compatible with Office 365 SMTP routing - it would require setting SMTP_HOST to smtp.office365.com, SMTP_USER to the admin Office 365 email address, and SMTP_PASS to the corresponding password or app-specific password. Port 587 is already configured correctly for Office 365's STARTTLS submission protocol.</narrative>

--- MODE SWITCH: PROGRESS SUMMARY ---
Do NOT output <observation> tags. This is a summary request, not an observation request.
Your response MUST use <summary> tags ONLY. Any <observation> output will be discarded.

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
Yes, Office 365 will work. The current SMTP setup in `forgot-password/route.ts:40` uses nodemailer with `host`, `port: 587`, and basic `auth: { user, pass }` — which is exactly how O365 SMTP relay works.

You'd set these env vars:

```
SMTP_HOST=smtp.office365.com
SMTP_USER=your-admin@yourdomain.com
SMTP_PASS=your-password
```

**One caveat:** Microsoft deprecated basic auth for O365 in 2022 for most tenants. Whether it works depends on your setup:

| Scenario | Works? |
|---|---|
| Microsoft 365 with **SMTP AUTH** enabled per-mailbox | Yes, as-is |
| Exchange Online with **SMTP client submission** (port 587) | Yes, if the mailbox has SMTP AUTH enabled |
| Tenant with **Security Defaults** or **Conditional Access** blocking basic auth | No — you'd need OAuth2 (XOAUTH2) |

**To check:** In the M365 admin center → Users → Active users → select the mailbox → Mail → Manage email apps → confirm "Authenticated SMTP" is checked.

If basic auth is blocked, the nodemailer transport would need to be switched to OAuth2, which is more involved. Want me to add that support?

Respond in this XML format:

  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

<summary>
  <request>Office 365 SMTP compatibility assessment for admin email routing in westcoast-marine-parks application</request>
  <investigated>Searched codebase for email/SMTP implementation patterns, finding 15 files with email functionality across authentication, admin, and queue systems. Examined environment variable configuration in .env.example and analyzed password reset email implementation in forgot-password route handler.</investigated>
  <learned>Application uses nodemailer with standard SMTP configuration via environment variables (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_USER, SMTP_PASS) on port 587. Implementation is compatible with Office 365 SMTP (smtp.office365.com) using basic authentication. However, Microsoft deprecated basic auth in 2022 for most tenants - compatibility depends on whether SMTP AUTH is enabled per-mailbox in M365 admin center or if tenant has Security Defaults/Conditional Access blocking basic auth. OAuth2 (XOAUTH2) implementation would be required as alternative if basic auth is blocked.</learned>
  <completed>Office 365 compatibility assessment completed. Identified that current implementation will work with O365 if SMTP AUTH is enabled for the mailbox, requiring only environment variable configuration (SMTP_HOST=smtp.office365.com, SMTP_USER=admin email, SMTP_PASS=password). Documented the caveat about M365 security policies potentially requiring OAuth2 implementation.</completed>
  <next_steps>Awaiting user decision on whether OAuth2 support should be added to handle Office 365 tenants with basic authentication disabled by security policies.</next_steps>
  <notes>Port 587 is already correctly configured for Office 365's STARTTLS submission protocol. Environment variables for SMTP configuration are currently missing from .env.example but are checked at runtime in the forgot-password route with fallback to console logging in development mode.</notes>
